description: Entity Designer is a state-machine orchestrator. It uses terminateAfter: false for Requirements Analyst (so it runs again after and can either show DRAFT questions or chain to Schema Designer). It uses terminateAfter: true for Schema Designer and Validator (their messages go directly to user).

example_1_new_entity_request.description: Condition 4: FunctionalRequirements absent — call Requirements Analyst with terminateAfter: false

subAgent.name: Entity Requirements Analyst
subAgent.message: Gather functional requirements for a new entity. User wants: 'track project milestones — checkpoints with a title, due date, and status'. Write FINAL if you have enough info, or DRAFT with up to 3 questions if you need clarification. Return when done.

example_2_after_requirements_draft.description: Condition 6: Requirements Analyst returned DRAFT — extract questions from FunctionalRequirements and show them with a responseForm (one text field per question)
example_2_after_requirements_draft.message: Before I design the schema, I need a few details:

questions.label: Should milestones have a status (e.g. Active, Completed, Cancelled) or just a done/not-done flag?
Should each milestone belong to a project? If so, is Projects already an entity in your system?
Do you want to assign milestones to a specific team member (link to Users)?

example_3_user_answered_questions.description: Condition 5: DRAFT in payload, user just answered questions — call Requirements Analyst again with terminateAfter: false

subAgent.name: Entity Requirements Analyst
subAgent.message: Update FunctionalRequirements with the user's answers. User said: 'Yes, status: Active/Completed/Cancelled. Link to Projects entity. Assign to a team member via Users.' Finalize to FINAL mode — we have enough to design. Return when done.

example_4_after_requirements_final.description: Condition 7: Requirements Analyst returned FINAL, SchemaDesign absent — immediately call Schema Designer with terminateAfter: true

subAgent.name: Entity Schema Designer
subAgent.message: Design the schema based on FunctionalRequirements in the payload. Research any FK targets first using Database Research Agent. Write SchemaDesign (Prototype + TableDefinition + ModificationType) and return.

example_5_user_approved_schema.description: Condition 1: User approved the schema ('create it') — call Schema Validator with terminateAfter: true

subAgent.name: Entity Schema Validator
subAgent.message: Validate the TableDefinition in SchemaDesign.TableDefinition. Check authorization, schema blocklist, naming conflicts, and structural validity. Return ValidationResult.

example_6_schema_change_request.description: Condition 2: User wants a schema change ('add a Notes field') — call Schema Designer with terminateAfter: true

subAgent.name: Entity Schema Designer
subAgent.message: Design the schema based on FunctionalRequirements in the payload. Research any FK targets first using Database Research Agent. Write SchemaDesign (Prototype + TableDefinition + ModificationType) and return. Also apply this change from the user: add a Notes column (long text, optional).

example_7_build_success.description: Condition 3: BuildResult present — report final success
example_7_build_success.message:
Your new entity is live!

- **Entity Name**: Project Milestones
- **Table**: `__mj_UDT.ProjectMilestones`
- **Columns**: 7 custom + 3 auto-managed (ID, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt)

You can start creating records immediately via the API or Explorer UI.

example_8_validation_failure.description: Condition 3: ValidationResult with failure — explain and ask how to proceed
example_8_validation_failure.message:
There's a problem with the schema:

**Error**: An entity named `Project Milestones` already exists in `__mj_UDT`.

Would you like to use a different name, or modify the existing entity instead?
